Upstream agents: Narrative Agent (Agent 02) verdict='unsupported_claim' (confidence=low); Alt-Data Verify Agent (Agent 03) verdict='insufficient_evidence_to_verify' (confidence=low); Surge-Short Agent (Agent 05) recommended_action='watch' (confidence=low); Risk Agent (Agent 06) all veto conditions false (confidence=high). Per §13.6 SOFT_REASONING, structural absence of expected news/filing/event corroboration for a 56% surge is bear-thesis evidence. The absence pattern is consistent with either (i) momentum-driven move without fundamental catalyst, or (ii) catalyst occurred post-cutoff and is invisible to pre-cutoff decision. Either way, the catalyst cannot be identified from available evidence. The bear thesis is SUPPORTED by negative fundamentals and macro headwind, but WEAKENED by incomplete information (20 post-cutoff news items, missing alternative-data adapters, no SEC filing confirmation). Confidence is LOW due to hindsight risk: if the 20 dropped news items contain a material disclosure (e.g., 8-K, press release, partnership announcement), the decision is retroactively contaminated. Per §5.17 surge-short integrity-veto exception, the absence of hard-to-fake corroboration (news, filing, alt-data) is NOT a reason to veto; it is the pump signal that motivates the short. Recommended action: WATCH pending post-cutoff news reconciliation and SEC filing confirmation. If corroborating catalyst evidence emerges (e.g., SEC 8-K, press release, analyst downgrade), the thesis is invalidated and position should not be entered. If the stock reverses sharply (>-10% from entry) or alternative-data evidence contradicts the bear thesis, exit immediately.
